title: Python ModuleNotFoundError: dependency not installed
log:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "app/main.py", line 4, in <module>
    import requests
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'requests'
make: *** [run] Error 1
expected_root_cause: A required third-party package (requests) is not installed in the environment, so importing it fails. The dependency is missing from the installed packages (not declared/installed, e.g. missing from requirements or the venv was not synced).

title: Node EADDRINUSE: port already in use
log:
Error: listen EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::3000
    at Server.setupListenHandle [as _listen2] (node:net:1872:16)
    at listenInCluster (node:net:1920:12)
npm ERR! code ELIFECYCLE
expected_root_cause: Port 3000 is already bound by another process, so the server cannot start. A previous/duplicate process is still listening on that port (or two services are configured to use the same port).

title: Java Maven OutOfMemoryError during test
log:
java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
  at java.base/java.util.Arrays.copyOf(Arrays.java:3537)
[ERROR] Tests run: 12, Failures: 0, Errors: 1
[ERROR] BUILD FAILURE
expected_root_cause: The JVM ran out of heap memory during the test run. The allocated heap (-Xmx) is too small for the workload, or code is holding/allocating more memory than available (excessive allocation or a memory leak).

title: Postgres connection refused
log:
psycopg2.OperationalError: could not connect to server: Connection refused
  Is the server running on host "localhost" (127.0.0.1) and accepting TCP/IP connections on port 5432?
pytest: 3 errors during collection
expected_root_cause: The application cannot reach the PostgreSQL server: nothing is listening on localhost:5432. The database is not running (or not started/reachable) at the configured host and port.

title: CI permission denied executing script
log:
/bin/bash: ./scripts/deploy.sh: Permission denied
The command './scripts/deploy.sh' failed with exit code 126
Process completed with exit code 126.
expected_root_cause: The deploy.sh script is not executable (missing the execute permission bit), so the shell refuses to run it. The file needs chmod +x or to be invoked via an interpreter.

title: Docker manifest not found
log:
Step 1/8 : FROM node:21.9.0-alpine
manifest for node:21.9.0-alpine not found: manifest unknown: manifest unknown
ERROR: failed to solve: node:21.9.0-alpine: not found
expected_root_cause: The base image tag node:21.9.0-alpine does not exist in the registry, so the pull fails. The image tag/version is wrong or was never published (nonexistent tag).

title: npm ERESOLVE peer dependency conflict
log:
npm ERR! code ERESOLVE
npm ERR! ERESOLVE unable to resolve dependency tree
npm ERR! While resolving: app@1.0.0
npm ERR! Found: react@18.2.0
npm ERR! Could not resolve dependency: peer react@^17.0.0 from react-some-lib@4.1.0
expected_root_cause: A package (react-some-lib) requires a peer dependency of react ^17, but the project has react 18 installed, so npm cannot resolve a compatible dependency tree. There is a version mismatch between an installed package and a dependency's peer requirement.

title: Go nil pointer dereference panic
log:
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x0 pc=0x4a8f12]
goroutine 1 [running]:
main.(*Service).Handle(0x0, ...)
  /app/service.go:42 +0x1a
exit status 2
expected_root_cause: The code dereferences a nil pointer (a *Service receiver is nil) at service.go:42, causing a runtime panic. A pointer/value was not initialized before use (missing nil check or uninitialized struct).

title: YAML parse error in CI config
log:
Error: while parsing a block mapping
  in ".github/workflows/ci.yml", line 14, column 7
expected <block end>, but found '<block mapping start>'
Invalid workflow file
expected_root_cause: The CI workflow YAML is malformed: a syntax/indentation error around line 14 breaks the block mapping, so the parser cannot read the file. The YAML indentation/structure is invalid.

title: Test assertion failure: wrong expected value
log:
FAILED tests/test_math.py::test_discount - assert 90 == 80
  where 90 = apply_discount(100, 0.1)
E       assert 90 == 80
1 failed, 7 passed in 0.21s
expected_root_cause: The test asserts apply_discount(100, 0.1) should equal 80, but it returns 90. Either the discount logic is wrong (computes 10% off as 90 when the test expects a different rule) or the test's expected value is incorrect — a mismatch between implementation and the asserted expectation.
